notice: Maintenance & failover truth: every notice declares whether it is a maintenance, drain, failover, or tenant-migration window, the exact window time / timezone / offset, the affected deployment scope and write classes, which queued publish-later or local-draft work survives (kept separate from successful hosted mutations), what stays local-safe, and any changed tenant / region / endpoint boundary. A notice reads as current only while it is active and its last refresh is current — otherwise it downgrades and names why, and never collapses into a generic degraded banner. Shell, activity history, CLI / headless inspect, diagnostics, and support exports read this record verbatim.
title: Merge-queue drain window
summary: Merge queue drains existing work while new managed writes queue or save locally.

affected_scope.scope_summary: Managed-cloud and enterprise merge queue and collaboration in the US-east region.

axes.summary: Region boundary unchanged during the drain.
Tenant boundary unchanged during the drain.

boundary_change.summary: No tenant, region, or endpoint boundary change; this is a clean drain.

blocked_writes.note: New merge enqueues queue for publish-later and replay when the drain completes.
Existing collaboration sessions finish; new presence writes wait for the drain to complete.
An in-progress provider publish is saved as a local draft and survives the drain.

succeeded_hosted_mutations.note: An approval landed before the drain began.

retained_local_safe_capabilities: Editing, saving, and local search continue.
Local Git commit and branch continue.
local_continuity.continuity_summary: Local-first work continues; queued and local-draft work survives the drain.
